title.zh: 卷二十四上 食貨志
title.raw: 卷二十四上 食貨志 Volume 24a: Treatise on Trade 1

sentences.zh: 洪範八政，一曰食，二曰貨。

translations.literal: In the Great Plan's eight policies, first is food, second is goods.
translations.idiomatic: Among the Great Plan's eight state priorities, food comes first and economic goods second.

sentences.zh: 食謂農殖嘉穀可食之物，貨謂布帛可衣，及金刀龜貝，所以分財布利通有無者也。

translations.literal: Food means cultivated grain and edible produce; goods mean cloth and silk for clothing, as well as metal, knives, turtle shell, and shells, by which wealth is distributed and surpluses and shortages are exchanged.
translations.idiomatic: Here 'food' means agricultural staples, while 'goods' includes textiles and exchange media that move wealth and balance scarcity.

sentences.zh: 二者，生民之本，興自神農之世。

translations.literal: These two are the basis of people's livelihood, rising from Shennong's age.
translations.idiomatic: These two pillars of livelihood date back to the era of Shennong.

sentences.zh: 「斲木為耜，煣木為耒，耒鲈之利以教天下」，而食足；

translations.literal: 'He cut wood to make plowshares, bent wood to make handles; with the benefit of plow and hoe he taught the world,' and food became sufficient;
translations.idiomatic: With the invention and spread of plowing tools, food security became possible;

sentences.zh: 「日中為市，致天下之民，聚天下之貨，交易而退，各得其所」，而貨通。

translations.literal: 'At midday they made markets, bringing the world's people and gathering the world's goods; they traded and departed, each obtaining what was needed,' and goods circulated.
translations.idiomatic: and with organized markets, people exchanged goods efficiently and each got what they needed.

sentences.zh: 食足貨通，然後國實民富，而教化成。

translations.literal: Only when food is sufficient and goods circulate do the state become solid, the people rich, and civil transformation succeed.
translations.idiomatic: When subsistence and exchange are both secure, the state stabilizes, prosperity grows, and governance can truly civilize.

sentences.zh: 黃帝以下「通其變，使民不倦」。

translations.literal: From the Yellow Emperor onward they 'adapted to change so people would not tire.'
translations.idiomatic: Since the Yellow Emperor, effective rulers adapted institutions so people would not be overburdened.

sentences.zh: 堯命四子以「敬授民時」，舜命后稷以「黎民祖飢」，是為政首。

translations.literal: Yao ordered the Four Sons to 'reverently deliver the seasons to the people'; Shun ordered Houji to relieve the people's hunger-this was first among governance tasks.
translations.idiomatic: Yao and Shun treated timekeeping and grain provision as top priorities of rule.

sentences.zh: 禹平洪水，定九州，制土田，各因所生遠近，賦入貢棐，楙遷有無，萬國作乂。

translations.literal: Yu controlled floods, fixed the nine provinces, regulated land by local conditions and distance, set taxes and tribute, shifted surplus and shortage, and the myriad states became orderly.
translations.idiomatic: After flood control, Yu built a territorial-fiscal system that matched local ecology and enabled interregional balancing.

sentences.zh: 殷周之盛，詩書所述，要在安民，富而教之。

translations.literal: In Shang and Zhou's flourishing, as narrated in Odes and Documents, the key was to settle the people, enrich them, and then educate them.
translations.idiomatic: Classical accounts of Shang and Zhou stress a sequence: stabilize, enrich, then instruct.

sentences.zh: 故易稱「天地之大德曰生，聖人之大寶曰位；

translations.literal: Therefore the Changes says: 'Heaven and Earth's great virtue is giving life; the great treasure of sages is position;
translations.idiomatic: As the Changes teaches, cosmic virtue gives life, and political office is the sage ruler's great trust;

sentences.zh: 何以守位曰仁，何以聚人曰財。」

translations.literal: what preserves position is benevolence; what gathers people is wealth.'
translations.idiomatic: benevolence secures legitimacy, and material resources gather and hold the populace.

sentences.zh: 財者，帝王所以聚人守位，養成群生，奉順天德，治國安民之本也。

translations.literal: Wealth is what emperors and kings use to gather people and keep position, nourish living beings, follow Heaven's virtue, and govern and settle the state.
translations.idiomatic: Fiscal capacity is thus foundational to humane governance, social reproduction, and political order.

sentences.zh: 故曰：「不患寡而患不均，不患貧而患不安；

translations.literal: So it is said: 'Do not fear fewness; fear unevenness. Do not fear poverty; fear instability;
translations.idiomatic: Hence the maxim: inequality and insecurity are more dangerous than simple scarcity.

sentences.zh: 蓋均亡貧，和亡寡，安亡傾。」

translations.literal: for with evenness there is no poverty, with harmony no fewness, with stability no collapse.'
translations.idiomatic: Equity, social harmony, and stability are what prevent systemic breakdown.

sentences.zh: 是以聖王域民，築城郭以居之，制廬井以均之，開市肆以通之，設庠序以教之；

translations.literal: Therefore sage kings settled the people by defining domains, building walls and cities, regulating hamlets and fields to equalize, opening markets to circulate, and establishing schools to teach.
translations.idiomatic: Sage statecraft combined spatial planning, market integration, and education into one social system.

sentences.zh: 士農工商，四民有業。

translations.literal: Scholars, farmers, artisans, and merchants-the four peoples each had occupations.
translations.idiomatic: Society was organized into four functional estates: scholar, farmer, artisan, merchant.

sentences.zh: 學以居位曰士，闢土殖穀曰農，作巧成器曰工，通財鬻貨曰商。

translations.literal: Those who studied to hold office were scholars; those who opened land and grew grain were farmers; those who made skill and crafted tools were artisans; those who moved wealth and sold goods were merchants.
translations.idiomatic: The categories were occupational and institutional, not merely status labels.

sentences.zh: 聖王量能授事，四民陳力受職，故朝亡廢官，邑亡敖民，地亡曠土。

translations.literal: Sage kings measured ability and assigned tasks; the four peoples offered strength and accepted office. Thus in court there were no idle offices, in towns no unruly people, and on land no wasted fields.
translations.idiomatic: By matching talent to function, they reduced bureaucratic idleness, social disorder, and agricultural waste.

sentences.zh: 理民之道，地著為本。

translations.literal: In governing the people, fixed settlement on land is the foundation.
translations.idiomatic: Territorial settlement was treated as the base of governance.

sentences.zh: 故必建步立畝，正其經界。

translations.literal: Therefore one must establish pace and mu, and correct boundaries.
translations.idiomatic: So units of measurement and clear land boundaries were indispensable.

sentences.zh: 六尺為步，步百為畝，畝百為夫，夫三為屋，屋三為井，井方一里，是為九夫。

translations.literal: Six chi make one pace; one hundred paces make one mu; one hundred mu make one fu; three fu make one wu; three wu make one well-field; the well is one li square, being nine fu.
translations.idiomatic: Land units scaled in a strict geometric hierarchy up to the one-li square well-field.

sentences.zh: 八家共之，各受私田百畝，公田十畝，是為八百八十畝，餘二十畝以為廬舍。

translations.literal: Eight households shared it; each received one hundred mu private field, plus ten mu public field, totaling eight hundred eighty mu, with twenty mu remaining for dwellings.
translations.idiomatic: In the classic system, eight households held private allotments plus common/public land and village residential space.

sentences.zh: 出入相友，守望相助，疾病則救，民是以和睦，而教化齊同，力役生產可得而平也。

translations.literal: Entering and leaving they befriended one another, in watch and defense they assisted one another, in sickness they rescued one another. Thus people were harmonious, teaching unified, and labor and production could be balanced.
translations.idiomatic: Mutual-aid institutions bound social, defensive, and economic life into a cohesive local community.

sentences.zh: 民受田，上田夫百畝，中田夫二百畝，下田夫三百畝。

translations.literal: When people received fields: upper fields gave one man one hundred mu, middle fields two hundred, lower fields three hundred.
translations.idiomatic: Field assignment varied by quality, with larger allotments for lower-yield land.

sentences.zh: 歲耕種者為不易上田；

translations.literal: Land cultivated yearly was unchanged upper field;
translations.idiomatic: Continuously cultivated plots were classed as upper fields;

sentences.zh: 休一歲者為一易中田；

translations.literal: land rested one year was once-rotated middle field;
translations.idiomatic: one-year rotation made middle fields;

sentences.zh: 休二歲者為再易下田，三歲更耕之，自爰其處。

translations.literal: land rested two years was twice-rotated lower field; in three years cultivation shifted again, each to its place.
translations.idiomatic: two-year rotation made lower fields, with cyclical reassignment over a three-year cycle.

sentences.zh: 農民戶人己受田，其家眾男為餘夫，亦以口受田如比。

translations.literal: After a farming household head had received land, additional adult males in the household were surplus men and also received land by population in the same ratio.
translations.idiomatic: Adult male laborers beyond the primary assignee received additional allotments proportionate to household size.

sentences.zh: 士工商家受田，五口乃當農夫一人。

translations.literal: Scholar, artisan, and merchant households receiving land counted five persons as equivalent to one farmer.
translations.idiomatic: Non-farming households were converted to agricultural-equivalent units for allocation purposes.

sentences.zh: 此謂平土可以為法者也。

translations.literal: This is what is called level land that can serve as legal model.
translations.idiomatic: This was the normative model for land that could be standardized in law.

sentences.zh: 若山林藪澤原陵淳鹵之地，各以肥磽多少為差。

translations.literal: As for mountains, forests, marshes, plains, hills, and saline lands, each was adjusted by relative fertility and barrenness.
translations.idiomatic: Non-arable and mixed terrains were assessed by productive potential and graded accordingly.

sentences.zh: 有賦有稅。

translations.literal: There were both tax and levy.
translations.idiomatic: The system distinguished regular tax from special levy.

sentences.zh: 稅謂公田什一及工商衡虞之入也。

translations.literal: Tax meant the one-tenth public field and revenues from craft and market controls.
translations.idiomatic: Tax included the public-field tithe and regulated commercial/industrial income.

sentences.zh: 賦共車馬甲兵士徒之役，充實府庫賜予之用。

translations.literal: Levy supplied chariots, horses, armor, weapons, and troop service, filling stores and funding grants.
translations.idiomatic: Levy financed military readiness and state treasuries.

sentences.zh: 稅給郊社宗廟百神之祀，天子奉養百官祿食庶事之費。

translations.literal: Tax funded suburban, altar, temple, and hundred-spirit sacrifices, plus the Son of Heaven's support of officials, salaries, and administrative expenses.
translations.idiomatic: Regular tax underwrote ritual obligations and core administrative payroll/expenditure.

sentences.zh: 民年二十受田，六十歸田。

translations.literal: People received fields at twenty and returned fields at sixty.
translations.idiomatic: Land tenure ran roughly from age twenty to sixty.

sentences.zh: 七十以上，上所養也；

translations.literal: Those above seventy were maintained by the state;
translations.idiomatic: Those over seventy were public dependents;

sentences.zh: 十歲以下，上所長也；

translations.literal: those below ten were those the state raised;
translations.idiomatic: children under ten were under state-protected nurture;

sentences.zh: 十一以上，上所強也。

translations.literal: those above eleven were those the state strengthened.
translations.idiomatic: from eleven onward, youths entered productive cultivation and discipline.

sentences.zh: 種穀必雜五種，以備災害。

translations.literal: Grain planting must mix five varieties to prepare for disasters.
translations.idiomatic: Crop diversity was required as risk management against famine shocks.

sentences.zh: 田中不得有樹，用妨五穀。

translations.literal: Fields must not have trees, lest they obstruct the five grains.
translations.idiomatic: Trees were barred from grain plots to protect staple yields.

sentences.zh: 力耕數耘，收穫如寇盜之至。

translations.literal: Plow hard and weed often; harvest as if bandits were arriving.
translations.idiomatic: Cultivation demanded urgency and vigilance, especially at harvest.

sentences.zh: 還廬樹桑，菜茹有畦，瓜瓠果蓏殖於疆易。

translations.literal: Returning to homesteads, plant mulberry; keep vegetable beds in furrows; grow melons, gourds, fruits, and vines along boundaries and paths.
translations.idiomatic: Household economies were diversified through mulberry, vegetables, and boundary horticulture.

sentences.zh: 雞豚狗彘毋失其時，女修蠶織，則五十可以衣帛，七十可以食肉。

translations.literal: Raise chickens, pigs, dogs, and swine in season; women practice sericulture and weaving-then by fifty one can wear silk, by seventy eat meat.
translations.idiomatic: With proper timing in livestock and textile work, households could secure comfort in old age.

sentences.zh: 在野曰廬，在邑曰里。

translations.literal: In the fields it is called lu; in settlement it is called li.
translations.idiomatic: Rural residence units differed by open-field versus clustered-settlement context.

sentences.zh: 五家為鄰，五鄰為里，四里為族，五族為黨，五黨為州，五州為鄉。

translations.literal: Five households make a neighborhood; five neighborhoods a li; four li a clan; five clans a dang; five dang a zhou; five zhou a xiang.
translations.idiomatic: Local organization scaled from five-household cells up through layered administrative communities.

sentences.zh: 鄉，萬二千五百戶也。

translations.literal: A xiang is twelve thousand five hundred households.
translations.idiomatic: A full township unit comprised 12,500 households.

sentences.zh: 鄰長位下士，自此以上，稍登一級，至鄉而為卿也。

translations.literal: The neighborhood chief held lower-scholar rank; above this each level rose one grade, reaching township as qing rank.
translations.idiomatic: Leadership rank increased stepwise from neighborhood head up to township-level noble-office status.

sentences.zh: 於里有序而鄉有庠。

translations.literal: Within li there was xu instruction hall, and in xiang there was xiang school.
translations.idiomatic: Village and township each had institutional schooling spaces.

sentences.zh: 序以明教，庠則行禮而視化焉。

translations.literal: The xu clarified teaching; the xiang practiced rites and observed transformation.
translations.idiomatic: One focused on instruction, the other on ritual socialization and moral effects.

sentences.zh: 春令民畢出在野，冬則畢入於邑。

translations.literal: In spring people all went out to the fields; in winter all entered the settlements.
translations.idiomatic: Seasonal migration between field labor and settled residence was systematized.

sentences.zh: 其詩曰：「四之日舉止，同我婦子，饁彼南具。」

translations.literal: Its poem says: 'On the fourth day we rise and move, with our wives and children, carrying meals to those in the southern fields.'
translations.idiomatic: As the Odes depicts, whole households coordinated around agricultural seasons.

sentences.zh: 又曰：「十月蟋蟀，入我床下，嗟我婦子，聿為改歲，入此室處。」

translations.literal: Another says: 'In the tenth month the crickets enter beneath my bed; alas my wife and children, now we change the year and come dwell in this room.'
translations.idiomatic: Another ode marks winter return to enclosed domestic life.

sentences.zh: 所以順陰陽，備寇賊，習禮文也。

translations.literal: This followed yin-yang rhythms, prepared against bandits, and practiced rites and culture.
translations.idiomatic: The seasonal pattern synchronized cosmology, security, and ritual cultivation.

sentences.zh: 春，秋出民，里胥平旦坐於右塾，鄰長坐於右塾，畢出然後歸，夕亦如之。

translations.literal: In spring and autumn when people went out, li scribes sat at dawn in right-side schools and neighborhood heads likewise; only after all had departed did they return, and in evening likewise.
translations.idiomatic: Local officials monitored collective movement at dawn and dusk during seasonal transitions.

sentences.zh: 入者必持薪樵，輕重相分，班白不提挈。

translations.literal: Those entering had to carry fuel wood, with loads divided by strength; gray-haired people did not carry loads.
translations.idiomatic: Entry obligations included fuel transport, but elders were exempt from heavy carriage.

sentences.zh: 冬，民既入，婦人同巷，相從夜績，女工一月得四十五日。

translations.literal: In winter, after people had entered, women of the same lane gathered for night spinning; women's craft gained forty-five workdays per month.
translations.idiomatic: Winter brought collective nighttime textile labor, maximizing women's productive time.

sentences.zh: 必相從者，所以省費燎火，同巧拙而合習俗也。

translations.literal: Gathering together reduced lamp-fuel expense, equalized skill differences, and unified local custom.
translations.idiomatic: Collective work lowered costs, diffused technique, and stabilized shared social norms.

sentences.zh: 男女有不得其所者，因相與歌詠，各言其傷。

translations.literal: If men or women lacked proper place, they sang together, each voicing grievance.
translations.idiomatic: Song functioned as a social feedback channel for misplacement and distress.

sentences.zh: 是月，餘子亦在于序室。

translations.literal: In this month, surplus youths were also in school chambers.
translations.idiomatic: Extra youths were placed in instructional spaces during the winter cycle.

sentences.zh: 八歲入小學，學六甲五方書計之事，始知室家長幼之節。

translations.literal: At eight they entered elementary school, learning six stems, directional scripts, and counting methods, first knowing household and age-hierarchy norms.
translations.idiomatic: Primary education began early with literacy/numeracy basics and family-hierarchy etiquette.

sentences.zh: 十五入大學，學先聖禮樂，而知朝廷君臣之禮。

translations.literal: At fifteen they entered higher school, learning rites and music of former sages, and knowing court rituals of ruler and minister.
translations.idiomatic: By adolescence, curriculum shifted to classical ritual-political training.

sentences.zh: 其有秀異者，移鄉學于庠序；

translations.literal: Those outstanding were moved from township schools to formal academies;
translations.idiomatic: Students of exceptional ability were promoted upward through institutional tiers;

sentences.zh: 庠序之異者，移國學于少學。

translations.literal: those outstanding in academies were moved to state school lesser college.
translations.idiomatic: and top academy students advanced to state-level preparatory colleges.

sentences.zh: 諸侯歲貢少學之異者於天子，學于大學，命曰造士。

translations.literal: Feudal lords annually presented exceptional lesser-college students to the Son of Heaven, to study at the grand college, called 'made scholars.'
translations.idiomatic: Annual tribute of elite students fed the imperial higher-education pipeline.

sentences.zh: 行同能偶，則別之以射，然後爵命焉。

translations.literal: Where conduct was alike and ability matched, they were distinguished by archery, and then granted rank and appointment.
translations.idiomatic: Final differentiation and appointment used ritualized archery as merit assessment.

sentences.zh: 孟春之月，群居者將散，行人振木鐸徇于路，以采詩，獻之大師，比其音律，以聞於天子。

translations.literal: In first spring month, as those living together were to disperse, officials shook wooden clappers through roads to collect poems, presenting them to the Grand Music Master who matched their tones and then reported to the Son of Heaven.
translations.idiomatic: At seasonal transition, officials collected popular songs, had them musically classified, and submitted them upward as governance intelligence.

sentences.zh: 故曰王者不窺牖戶而知天下。

translations.literal: Therefore it is said: the king need not peek through windows to know the world.
translations.idiomatic: Hence the saying that a ruler can know the realm without private spying.

sentences.zh: 此先王制土處民富而教之之大略也。

translations.literal: This is the broad outline of how former kings regulated land, settled people, enriched and taught them.
translations.idiomatic: This summarizes classical land-population governance: settle, enrich, educate.

sentences.zh: 故孔子曰：「道千乘之國，敬事而信，節用而愛人，使民以時。」

translations.literal: Therefore Confucius said: 'Guide a thousand-chariot state by reverent service and trust, frugality in expenditure and love of people, employing the people seasonally.'
translations.idiomatic: Confucius' formula links trustworthy administration, fiscal restraint, and timed labor mobilization.

sentences.zh: 故民皆勸功樂業，先公而後私。

translations.literal: Thus people all encouraged achievement and delighted in work, placing public before private.
translations.idiomatic: Under such governance, productivity and civic orientation rose together.

sentences.zh: 其詩曰：「有渰淒淒，興雲祁祁，雨我公田，遂及我私。」

translations.literal: Its poem says: 'Dense clouds gather and rise; rain falls on our public fields and then reaches our private fields.'
translations.idiomatic: The ode captures the desired order: public obligation fulfilled first, private gain following.

sentences.zh: 民三年耕，則餘一年之畜。

translations.literal: With three years' cultivation, people then had one year's surplus stores.
translations.idiomatic: After sustained stable farming, households accumulated reserve grain.

sentences.zh: 衣食足而知榮辱，廉讓生而爭訟息，故三載考績。

translations.literal: When clothing and food are sufficient, people know honor and shame; when integrity and yielding arise, litigation ceases; therefore performance was assessed every three years.
translations.idiomatic: Material security enables moral culture, and moral culture reduces litigation-this justified triennial evaluation.

sentences.zh: 孔子曰「苟有用我者，期月而已可也，三年有成」，成此功也。

translations.literal: Confucius said, 'If someone employed me, in one year it could be workable, in three years there would be achievement'-this achievement is this.
translations.idiomatic: Confucius' 'three years to achievement' points to precisely this socio-economic-moral consolidation.

sentences.zh: 三考黜陟，餘三年食，進業曰登；

translations.literal: Across three evaluations with promotion and demotion, after surplus three-year food, one advance is called deng;
translations.idiomatic: Advancement stages were tied to repeated performance cycles and surplus capacity;

sentences.zh: 再登曰平，餘六年食；

translations.literal: second advance called ping, with six years' surplus food;
translations.idiomatic: second-stage success yielded six years' reserve;

sentences.zh: 三登曰泰平，二十七歲，遺九年食。

translations.literal: third advance called taiping, at twenty-seven years, leaving nine years' food.
translations.idiomatic: third-stage maturity produced deep multi-year surplus associated with great peace.

sentences.zh: 然後鲈德流洽，禮樂成焉。

translations.literal: Then virtue flows and saturates, and rites and music are completed.
translations.idiomatic: Only then can moral influence fully permeate and ritual-culture stabilize.

sentences.zh: 故曰「如有王者，必世而後仁」，繇此道也。」

translations.literal: Therefore 'if there is a true king, a generation then benevolence'-it is by this path.
translations.idiomatic: This is the pathway behind the saying that true benevolent order takes a generation.

sentences.zh: 周室既衰，暴君污吏慢其經界，繇役橫作，政令不信，上下相詐，公田不治。

translations.literal: After Zhou house declined, violent rulers and corrupt officials neglected boundaries, labor service arose arbitrarily, policies lacked trust, superiors and inferiors deceived each other, and public fields were not cultivated.
translations.idiomatic: With Zhou decline, boundary order and labor governance collapsed, producing mutual mistrust and agrarian decay.

sentences.zh: 故魯宣公「初稅畝」，春秋譏焉。

translations.literal: So Lu's Duke Xuan first taxed per mu, and Spring and Autumn criticized it.
translations.idiomatic: The Annals condemns Lu's shift to per-mu tax as a symptom of this decline.

sentences.zh: 於是上貪民怨，災害生而禍亂作。

translations.literal: Thus superiors became greedy and people resentful, disasters arose and calamities and disorder occurred.
translations.idiomatic: Fiscal predation triggered resentment, then environmental and political crisis.

sentences.zh: 陵夷至於戰國，貴詐力而賤仁誼，先富有而後禮讓。

translations.literal: Declining to Warring States, they valued fraud and force and cheapened benevolence and righteousness, putting wealth and possession before ritual yielding.
translations.idiomatic: In the Warring States ethos, power and acquisition displaced moral and ritual priorities.

sentences.zh: 是時，李悝為魏文侯作盡地力之教，以為地方百里，提封九萬頃，除山澤邑居參分去一，為田六百萬畝，治田勤謹則畝益三升，不勤則損亦如之。

translations.literal: Then Li Kui for Marquis Wen of Wei made teachings of exhausting land power: in a hundred-li square domain with ninety-thousand qing under seal, subtracting mountains, marshes, and settlements by one-third leaves six million mu; diligent cultivation adds three sheng per mu, negligence loses the same.
translations.idiomatic: Li Kui's program quantified agricultural output gains from intensified cultivation within a calibrated land-assessment framework.

sentences.zh: 地方百里之增減，輒為粟百八十萬石矣。

translations.literal: For a hundred-li square, such increase or decrease makes one million eight hundred thousand shi of grain.
translations.idiomatic: At that scale, marginal yield changes translated into enormous aggregate grain differences.

sentences.zh: 又曰糴其貴傷民，甚賤傷農；

translations.literal: He also said: if grain purchases are too expensive, people are harmed; if very cheap, farmers are harmed;
translations.idiomatic: He argued that both price spikes and price collapses are socially destructive.

sentences.zh: 民傷則離散，農傷則國貧。

translations.literal: if people are harmed they scatter; if farmers are harmed the state is poor.
translations.idiomatic: High prices disintegrate society; low prices impoverish the agrarian base.

sentences.zh: 故甚貴與甚賤，其傷一也。

translations.literal: So extreme high and extreme low both harm equally.
translations.idiomatic: Either pricing extreme damages state capacity in different ways.

sentences.zh: 善為國者，使民毋傷而農益勸。

translations.literal: Good statecraft lets people not be harmed and farmers become more encouraged.
translations.idiomatic: The policy goal is dual protection of consumers and producers.

sentences.zh: 今一夫挾五口，治田百畝，歲收畝一石半，為粟百五十石，除十一之稅十五石，餘百三十五石。

translations.literal: Now one man supports five mouths, cultivating one hundred mu, yearly harvesting one and a half shi per mu, making one hundred fifty shi; removing one-tenth tax fifteen shi, remaining one hundred thirty-five.
translations.idiomatic: Li Kui's household arithmetic starts from a five-person household farming one hundred mu under average yield and tithe assumptions.

sentences.zh: 食，人月一石半，五人終歲為粟九十石，餘有四十五石。

translations.literal: Food use is one and a half shi per person per month; five persons for a full year use ninety shi, leaving forty-five.
translations.idiomatic: After subsistence consumption, only a narrow surplus remains.

sentences.zh: 石三十，為錢千三百五十，除社閭嘗新春秋之祠，用錢三百，餘千五十。

translations.literal: At thirty shi valued as 1,350 cash; removing village and sacrificial expenses of 300 cash leaves 1,050.
translations.idiomatic: Converting surplus to cash and deducting communal ritual obligations sharply reduces disposable income.

sentences.zh: 衣，人率用錢三百，五人終歲用千五百，不足四百五十。

translations.literal: Clothing costs average three hundred cash per person, five persons yearly fifteen hundred, short by four hundred fifty.
translations.idiomatic: Textile expenses alone push the household into deficit.

sentences.zh: 不幸疾病死喪之費，及上賦斂，又未與此。

translations.literal: Unlucky illness, death, funeral expenses, and higher levies are not yet included here.
translations.idiomatic: And this excludes shocks like illness, funerals, and extra exactions.

sentences.zh: 此農夫所以常困，有不勸耕之心，而令糴至於甚貴者也。

translations.literal: This is why farmers are constantly distressed, with unwillingness to cultivate, and grain prices reach extreme high.
translations.idiomatic: Persistent peasant hardship undermines cultivation incentives and fuels price instability.

sentences.zh: 是故善平糴者，必謹觀歲有上中下孰。

translations.literal: Therefore those good at leveling grain must carefully observe whether the year is high, medium, or low harvest.
translations.idiomatic: Effective price-stabilization policy requires accurate harvest classification.

sentences.zh: 上孰其收自四，餘四百石；

translations.literal: In a high harvest, return is fourfold, surplus four hundred shi;
translations.idiomatic: In bumper years, yields create roughly four-hundred-shi surplus;

sentences.zh: 中孰自三，餘三百石；

translations.literal: in a medium harvest, return is threefold, surplus three hundred shi;
translations.idiomatic: in middling years, surplus falls to about three hundred shi.

sentences.zh: 下孰自倍，餘百石。

translations.literal: In a poor harvest, return is doubled, leaving one hundred shi surplus.
translations.idiomatic: In low-yield years, only about one hundred shi remain in surplus.

sentences.zh: 小飢則收百石，中飢七十石，大飢三十石。

translations.literal: In a minor famine, collect one hundred shi; in moderate famine seventy; in major famine thirty.
translations.idiomatic: Reserve-release volumes should scale with famine severity: 100, 70, then 30 shi.

sentences.zh: 故大孰則上糴三而舍一，中孰則糴二，下孰則糴一，使民適足，賈平則止。

translations.literal: Thus in a great harvest buy at ratio three and store one, in medium harvest buy two, in low harvest buy one; let people be just sufficient, and stop when prices are level.
translations.idiomatic: In good years the state should purchase and store grain proportionately, then stop once market prices are stabilized.

sentences.zh: 小飢則發小孰之所斂，中飢則發中孰之所斂，大飢則發大孰之所斂，而糶之。

translations.literal: In minor famine release what was gathered in minor years; in medium famine release medium-year stores; in major famine release major-year stores and sell.
translations.idiomatic: Famine response should draw from reserves accumulated under matching prior conditions.

sentences.zh: 故雖遇饑饉水旱，糴不貴而民不散，取有餘以補不足也。

translations.literal: Therefore even when famine, flood, or drought occurs, grain does not become expensive and people do not scatter-taking surplus to fill shortage.
translations.idiomatic: Countercyclical grain policy prevents both price spikes and population flight.

sentences.zh: 行之魏國，國以富彊。

translations.literal: Applied in Wei state, the state became rich and strong.
translations.idiomatic: Implemented in Wei, this policy produced prosperity and military strength.

sentences.zh: 及秦孝公用商君，壞井田，開仟伯，急耕戰之賞，雖非古道，猶以務本之故，傾鄰國而雄諸侯。

translations.literal: When Duke Xiao of Qin used Lord Shang, he broke well-fields, opened boundaries, and hastened rewards for farming and war. Though not the ancient way, by attending to fundamentals he overwhelmed neighboring states and dominated lords.
translations.idiomatic: Shang Yang's reforms were anti-classical but materially effective: they concentrated agrarian output and military mobilization.

sentences.zh: 然王制遂滅，僭差亡度。

translations.literal: Yet kingly institutions were then destroyed, and usurpation and deviation lost all measure.
translations.idiomatic: But institutional legitimacy and ritual limits collapsed in the process.

sentences.zh: 庶人之富者累鉅萬，而貧者食糟糠；

translations.literal: Among commoners, the rich accumulated tens of thousands, while the poor ate husks and bran;
translations.idiomatic: Wealth polarization intensified: elites amassed fortunes while many survived on coarse scraps.

sentences.zh: 有國彊者兼州域，而弱者喪社稷。

translations.literal: among states, the strong annexed territories, while the weak lost altars and shrines.
translations.idiomatic: Likewise, interstate inequality ended in annexation and regime extinction.

sentences.zh: 至於始皇，遂并天下，內興功作，外攘夷狄，收泰半之賦，發閭左之戍。

translations.literal: By First Emperor, he annexed all under heaven, built projects internally, repelled barbarians externally, took more than half the tax, and drafted left-register households for garrison.
translations.idiomatic: Qin unification paired massive extraction and labor mobilization with frontier militarization and megaprojects.

sentences.zh: 男子力耕不足糧饟，女子紡績不足衣服。

translations.literal: Men's hard plowing was insufficient for grain supplies; women's spinning and weaving insufficient for clothing.
translations.idiomatic: Household labor could no longer meet subsistence under state demands.

sentences.zh: 竭天下之資財以奉其政，猶未足以澹其欲也。

translations.literal: Exhausting the realm's resources to support his governance still did not suffice to satisfy his desires.
translations.idiomatic: Even total fiscal extraction failed to satisfy imperial overreach.

sentences.zh: 海內愁怨，遂用潰畔。

translations.literal: The whole realm grieved and resented, and thus collapsed into rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: General resentment broke into systemic revolt.

sentences.zh: 筦子曰「倉廩實而知禮節」。

translations.literal: Guanzi says: 'When granaries are full, people know ritual propriety.'
translations.idiomatic: As Guanzi says, ritual order depends on material sufficiency.

sentences.zh: 民不足而可治者，自古及今，未之嘗聞。

translations.literal: That people lacking means can still be governed-from ancient to present, this has never been heard.
translations.idiomatic: No regime has stably governed a population in chronic deprivation.

sentences.zh: 古之人曰：「一夫不耕，或受之飢；

translations.literal: Ancients said: 'If one man does not farm, someone suffers hunger;
translations.idiomatic: The old maxim links each missing producer to downstream deprivation;

sentences.zh: 一女不織，或受之寒。」

translations.literal: if one woman does not weave, someone suffers cold.'
translations.idiomatic: each missing spinner leaves someone unclothed.

sentences.zh: 生之有時，而用之亡度，則物力必屈。

translations.literal: Production has its timing, but use has no measure-then productive force must collapse.
translations.idiomatic: If consumption outruns seasonal production, the economy inevitably buckles.

sentences.zh: 古之治天下，至孅至悉也，故其畜積足恃。

translations.literal: Ancient governance of the realm was meticulous to the finest degree; therefore their stores were sufficient to rely on.
translations.idiomatic: Classical states built high-resolution management, which is why reserves were dependable.

sentences.zh: 今背本而趨末，食者甚眾，是天下之大殘也；

translations.literal: Now people turn away from root and rush to branch, while consumers are many-this is the realm's great injury;
translations.idiomatic: When society abandons productive fundamentals for secondary pursuits, macroeconomic damage follows.

sentences.zh: 淫侈之俗，日日以長，是天下之大賊也。

translations.literal: and luxurious, extravagant custom grows daily-this is the realm's great thief.
translations.idiomatic: Escalating luxury culture is treated as parasitic extraction from real production.

sentences.zh: 殘賊公行，莫之或止；

translations.literal: Injury and theft run openly, with none to stop them;
translations.idiomatic: Predatory behavior has become normalized and unchecked;

sentences.zh: 大命將泛，莫之振救。

translations.literal: the great mandate is about to drift, with none to rescue and stabilize it.
translations.idiomatic: political legitimacy itself is nearing systemic failure.

sentences.zh: 生之者甚少而靡之者甚多，天下財產何得不蹶！

translations.literal: Those who produce are very few while those who consume are very many-how can the realm's wealth not collapse!
translations.idiomatic: A widening producer-consumer imbalance makes fiscal collapse unavoidable.

sentences.zh: 漢之為漢幾四十年矣，公私之積猶可哀痛。

translations.literal: Han has been Han nearly forty years, yet public and private accumulations are still pitiably painful.
translations.idiomatic: Even after decades, both state and household reserves remained fragile.

sentences.zh: 失時不雨，民且狼顧；

translations.literal: If seasonal rain fails, people already look about like wolves;
translations.idiomatic: Any weather shock immediately triggers social anxiety and scarcity behavior;

sentences.zh: 歲惡不入，請賣爵、子。

translations.literal: if bad harvest yields no intake, they request selling ranks and even children.
translations.idiomatic: In severe scarcity, people seek desperate liquidity-including status sales and family liquidation.

sentences.zh: 既聞耳矣，安有為天下阽危者若是而上不驚者！

translations.literal: We have already heard this-how can the realm be in such peril and those above not be alarmed!
translations.idiomatic: Given such warning signs, elite complacency is inexcusable.

sentences.zh: 世之有飢穰，天之行也，禹、湯被之矣。

translations.literal: Ages have famine and abundance-this is Heaven's operation; Yu and Tang experienced it too.
translations.idiomatic: Famine cycles are natural and recurrent even under great rulers.

sentences.zh: 即不幸有方二三千里之旱，國胡以相恤？

translations.literal: If by misfortune there is drought over two or three thousand li, how can the state mutually relieve it?
translations.idiomatic: Large-scale drought requires coordinated interregional relief capacity.

sentences.zh: 卒然邊境有急，數十百萬之眾，國胡以餽之？

translations.literal: If suddenly borders are urgent and crowds of hundreds of thousands arise, how can the state provision them?
translations.idiomatic: Likewise, emergency mobilization needs strategic grain depth.

sentences.zh: 兵旱相乘，天下大屈，有勇力者聚徒而衡擊，罷夫羸老易子而沦其骨。

translations.literal: When war and drought strike together, the world greatly bends: those with strength gather bands and attack; exhausted elders exchange children and gnaw their bones.
translations.idiomatic: Compound shocks of war plus famine produce social violence and extreme famine behaviors.

sentences.zh: 政治未畢通也，遠方之能疑者並舉而爭起矣，乃駭而圖之，豈將有及乎？

translations.literal: If governance has not yet been fully connected, distant powers still able to doubt will all rise and contend; then to panic and plan, how could there be time?
translations.idiomatic: Without integrated governance beforehand, crisis response comes too late.

sentences.zh: 夫積貯者，天下之大命也。

translations.literal: Accumulated storage is the great lifeline of the realm.
translations.idiomatic: Strategic reserves are the state's core survival mechanism.

sentences.zh: 苟粟多而財有餘，何為而不成？

translations.literal: If grain is abundant and wealth in surplus, what objective cannot be accomplished?
translations.idiomatic: With deep reserves, nearly all strategic aims become feasible.

sentences.zh: 以攻則取，以守則固，以戰則勝。

translations.literal: To attack, one takes; to defend, one is firm; to fight, one wins.
translations.idiomatic: Reserves convert directly into offensive, defensive, and battlefield advantage.

sentences.zh: 懷敵附遠，何招而不至？

translations.literal: Holding enemies and attaching distant peoples-what summons would not come?
translations.idiomatic: Material strength also enhances diplomacy and long-distance attraction.

sentences.zh: 今毆民而歸之農，皆著於本，使天下各食其力，末技游食之民轉而緣南畝，則畜積足而人樂其所矣。

translations.literal: Now if we drive people back to agriculture, all fixed on the root, so everyone eats by labor and roaming eaters of secondary skills turn toward southern furrows, then stores suffice and people enjoy their place.
translations.idiomatic: Re-agrarianization and labor reallocation toward primary production would stabilize both reserves and social satisfaction.

sentences.zh: 可以為富安天下，而直為此廩廩也，竊為陛下惜之！

translations.literal: This could enrich and secure the realm, yet we only remain trembling in fear-I secretly regret this for Your Majesty.
translations.idiomatic: The policy path to security is available, making current anxiety feel self-inflicted.

sentences.zh: 於是上感誼言，始開籍田，躬耕以勸百姓。

translations.literal: Thereupon the emperor was moved by Jia Yi's words, first opened imperial fields and personally plowed to encourage the people.
translations.idiomatic: Jia Yi's argument influenced symbolic imperial plowing to promote agriculture.

sentences.zh: 晁錯復說上曰：

translations.literal: Chao Cuo again persuaded the emperor, saying:
translations.idiomatic: Chao Cuo then offered further policy argument:

sentences.zh: 聖王在上而民不凍飢者，非能耕而食之，織而衣之也，為開其資財之道也。

translations.literal: When a sage king is above and people are not cold or hungry, it is not because he personally plows for them or weaves for them, but because he opens channels for their resources.
translations.idiomatic: Rulers prevent destitution by institutional design, not by direct personal production.

sentences.zh: 故堯、禹有九年之水，湯有七年之旱，而國亡捐瘠者，以畜積多而備先具也。

translations.literal: Therefore Yao and Yu had nine years of flood and Tang had seven years of drought, yet the state had no abandoned emaciated people, because stores were many and preparation made in advance.
translations.idiomatic: Historical resilience came from pre-positioned reserves, not crisis improvisation.

sentences.zh: 今海內為一，土地人民之眾不避湯、禹，加以亡天災數年之水旱，而畜積未及者，何也？

translations.literal: Now with the realm unified, land and population not less than Tang or Yu, and with no multi-year flood or drought, why have accumulations not reached them?
translations.idiomatic: Given favorable baseline conditions, weak reserves indicate policy failure rather than natural destiny.

sentences.zh: 地有遺利，民有餘力，生穀之土未盡墾，山澤之利未盡出也，游食之民未盡歸農也。

translations.literal: Land has leftover benefit, people have surplus strength, grain-growing land not fully opened, mountain and marsh resources not fully extracted, and roaming eaters not fully returned to farming.
translations.idiomatic: Untapped land, labor, and resource capacity still remained, along with too much nonproductive population.

sentences.zh: 民貧，則姦邪生。

translations.literal: When people are poor, then wickedness arises.
translations.idiomatic: Poverty is a direct driver of criminality.

sentences.zh: 貧生於不足，不足生於不農，不農則不地著，不地著則離鄉輕家，民如鳥獸，雖有高城深池，嚴法重刑，猶不能禁也。

translations.literal: Poverty comes from insufficiency, insufficiency from non-farming, non-farming from not being fixed to land; if not fixed, they leave villages and treat homes lightly, like birds and beasts-even with high walls, deep moats, strict law, and heavy punishment, one still cannot restrain them.
translations.idiomatic: Without land attachment and agrarian livelihood, coercive control alone cannot sustain order.

sentences.zh: 夫寒之於衣，不待輕煖；

translations.literal: For cold toward clothing, one does not wait for light warmth;
translations.idiomatic: When freezing, people need any clothing, not luxury warmth;

sentences.zh: 飢之於食，不待甘旨；

translations.literal: for hunger toward food, one does not wait for delicacies.
translations.idiomatic: when starving, any calories matter more than fine cuisine.

sentences.zh: 飢寒至身，不顧廉恥。

translations.literal: When hunger and cold reach the body, one does not care for shame or honor.
translations.idiomatic: Extreme deprivation overwhelms moral restraint.

sentences.zh: 人情，一日不再食則飢，終歲不製衣則寒。

translations.literal: Human condition: one day without a second meal means hunger; one full year without making clothing means cold.
translations.idiomatic: Basic physiological thresholds are immediate and unforgiving.

sentences.zh: 夫腹飢不得食，膚寒不得衣，雖慈母不能保其子，君安能以有其民哉！

translations.literal: If the belly is hungry without food and skin cold without clothes, even a loving mother cannot keep her child-how can a ruler then keep his people?
translations.idiomatic: If subsistence fails, even strongest natural bonds weaken; state loyalty cannot survive either.

sentences.zh: 明主知其然也，故務民於農桑，薄賦斂，廣畜積，以實倉廩，備水旱，故民可得而有也。

translations.literal: A wise ruler knows this, therefore urges people toward agriculture and mulberry, lightens taxes and levies, broadens storage to fill granaries and prepare for flood and drought-only then can people be retained.
translations.idiomatic: Hence pro-farming policy, light extraction, and grain reserves are the true basis of durable rule.

sentences.zh: 民者，在上所以牧之，趨利如水走下，四方亡擇也。

translations.literal: The people are what those above herd; they run toward profit like water downhill, choosing no direction.
translations.idiomatic: Human behavior follows incentive gradients; policy must account for that.

sentences.zh: 夫珠玉金銀，飢不可食，寒不可衣，然而眾貴之者，以上用之故也。

translations.literal: Pearls, jade, gold, and silver cannot be eaten when hungry nor worn when cold, yet people value them because superiors use them.
translations.idiomatic: Elite preference confers social value even on non-subsistence goods.

sentences.zh: 其為物輕微易臧，在於把握，可以周海內而亡飢寒之患。

translations.literal: These goods are light, subtle, and easy to hide, fitting in one grasp, and can circulate the realm without hunger or cold burden.
translations.idiomatic: Portable high-value goods enable rapid exchange but also easy concealment and mobility.

sentences.zh: 此令臣輕背其主，而民易去其鄉，盜賊有所勸，亡逃者得輕資也。

translations.literal: This makes ministers easy to betray lords, people easy to leave hometowns, thieves encouraged, and runaways able to carry light assets.
translations.idiomatic: Liquid wealth lowers exit costs and can amplify political disloyalty and criminal mobility.

sentences.zh: 粟米布帛生於地，長於時，聚於力，非可一日成也；

translations.literal: Grain and cloth are born from land, grow with seasons, and gather by labor-they cannot be made in one day;
translations.idiomatic: Staple goods are slow, labor-bound, and season-dependent;

sentences.zh: 數石之重，中人弗勝，不為姦邪所利，一日弗得而飢寒至。

translations.literal: their several-stone weight ordinary persons cannot carry, they do not benefit wrongdoing, and if one day not obtained, hunger and cold arrive.
translations.idiomatic: because staples are bulky and non-portable, they are less suited to illicit flight and remain tied to local livelihood.

sentences.zh: 是故明君貴五穀而賤金玉。

translations.literal: Therefore wise rulers value the five grains and devalue gold and jade.
translations.idiomatic: So policy should prioritize staple grain over prestige valuables.

sentences.zh: 今農夫五口之家，其服役者不下二人，其能耕者不過百畝，百畝之收不過百石。

translations.literal: Now in a five-person farming household, no fewer than two serve labor duties; what they can cultivate is no more than one hundred mu, whose yield no more than one hundred shi.
translations.idiomatic: Typical farm households carry labor obligations that cap cultivable area and output.

sentences.zh: 春耕夏耘，秋穫冬臧，伐薪樵，治官府，給繇役；

translations.literal: Spring plow, summer weed, autumn harvest, winter store; cut firewood, serve offices, and supply corvée labor;
translations.idiomatic: Their annual cycle combines farming with state labor and service obligations.

sentences.zh: 春不得避風塵，夏不得避暑熱，秋不得避陰雨，冬不得避寒凍，四時之間亡日休息；

translations.literal: In spring they cannot avoid wind and dust, in summer heat, in autumn rain, in winter cold-no day of rest through the four seasons;
translations.idiomatic: Agrarian labor is physically continuous across extreme seasonal conditions.

sentences.zh: 又私自送往迎來，弔死問疾，養孤長幼在其中。

translations.literal: and privately they still must conduct gifts and receptions, mourn dead and visit sick, raise orphans and young and old within this.
translations.idiomatic: On top of production, households bear dense social and kin obligations.

sentences.zh: 勤苦如此，尚復被水旱之災，急政暴虐，賦斂不時，朝令而暮改。

translations.literal: Though so hardworking, they still suffer flood and drought, urgent policy and violent abuse, untimely levies, edicts issued at dawn and changed by dusk.
translations.idiomatic: Despite extreme effort, policy volatility and extraction shocks keep peasants structurally vulnerable.

sentences.zh: 當具有者半賈而賣，亡者取倍稱之息，於是有賣田宅鬻子孫以償責者矣。

translations.literal: When needing complete resources they sell at half price; when lacking they borrow at double interest; thus some sell fields and houses and even children and descendants to repay debt.
translations.idiomatic: Credit asymmetry and distress sales drive households into debt peonage and asset liquidation.

sentences.zh: 而商賈大者積貯倍息，小者坐列販賣，操其奇贏，日游都市，乘上之急，所賣必倍。

translations.literal: Meanwhile large merchants store and lend at doubled interest; small merchants sit in rows trading, manipulating unusual profit, daily roaming markets, exploiting state urgency, and selling at doubled price.
translations.idiomatic: Commercial actors captured crisis rents at multiple scales, from usury to speculative markups.

sentences.zh: 故其男不耕耘，女不蠶織，衣必文采，食必梁肉；

translations.literal: Thus their men do not plow, their women do not spin, yet clothes must be patterned silk and food polished grain and meat;
translations.idiomatic: Merchant households consumed luxuriously while detached from direct production.

sentences.zh: 亡農夫之苦，有仟伯之得。

translations.literal: without farmers' hardship, they gain tenfold and hundredfold.
translations.idiomatic: They captured large returns without bearing agrarian risk.

sentences.zh: 因其富厚，交通王侯，力過吏勢，以利相傾；

translations.literal: Relying on their wealth, they connect with princes and lords, power surpassing officials, tilting by profit against one another.
translations.idiomatic: Accumulated capital translated into political influence beyond formal office.

sentences.zh: 千里游敖，冠蓋相望，乘堅策肥，履絲曳縞。

translations.literal: Traveling thousands of li in leisure, canopied carriages in view of one another, riding hard carriages and fat horses, treading silk and trailing gauze.
translations.idiomatic: Elite merchant mobility and conspicuous consumption signaled a parallel power structure.

sentences.zh: 此商人所以兼并農人，農人所以流亡者也。

translations.literal: This is why merchants annex farmers, and why farmers become displaced and fugitive.
translations.idiomatic: The result is agrarian dispossession and rural flight.

sentences.zh: 今法律賤商人，商人已富貴矣；

translations.literal: Now laws despise merchants, yet merchants are already rich and noble;
translations.idiomatic: Statutory rhetoric condemns merchants, but material outcomes reward them;

sentences.zh: 尊農夫，農夫已貧賤矣。

translations.literal: they honor farmers, yet farmers are already poor and low.
translations.idiomatic: while proclaimed agrarian priority coexists with peasant impoverishment.

sentences.zh: 故俗之所貴，主之所賤也；

translations.literal: Thus what custom values is what rulers devalue;
translations.idiomatic: Social prestige and state ideology diverge;

sentences.zh: 吏之所卑，法之所尊也。

translations.literal: what officials treat as low is what law exalts.
translations.idiomatic: bureaucratic practice and legal text pull in opposite directions.

sentences.zh: 上下相反，好惡乖迕，而欲國富法立，不可得也。

translations.literal: Upper and lower oppose one another, likes and dislikes conflict; seeking state wealth and legal authority then cannot be achieved.
translations.idiomatic: Without incentive alignment, neither fiscal strength nor legal order can be sustained.

sentences.zh: 方今之務，莫若使民務農而已矣。

translations.literal: At present, no task is better than making people devote themselves to farming.
translations.idiomatic: Current priority should be straightforward: re-center agriculture.

sentences.zh: 欲民務農，在於貴粟；

translations.literal: To make people devote themselves to farming lies in valuing grain;
translations.idiomatic: To do that, grain must be made comparatively valuable;

sentences.zh: 貴粟之道，在於使民以粟為賞罰。

translations.literal: the way to value grain lies in making grain the medium of rewards and punishments.
translations.idiomatic: and grain must become a core instrument in both incentives and sanctions.

sentences.zh: 今募天下入粟縣官，得以拜爵，得以除罪。

translations.literal: Now recruit all under heaven to submit grain to county officials, and allow gaining rank and removing crimes.
translations.idiomatic: Policy proposal: accept grain payments in exchange for rank advancement and penal remission.

sentences.zh: 如此，富人有爵，農民有錢，粟有所渫。

translations.literal: If so, rich people gain rank, farmers gain money, and grain has outlet.
translations.idiomatic: This would create channels where surplus grain is monetized and socially rewarded.

sentences.zh: 夫能入粟以受爵，皆有餘者也；

translations.literal: Those able to submit grain to receive rank are all those with surplus;
translations.idiomatic: Those paying in grain are precisely households with excess production;

sentences.zh: 取於有餘，以供上用，則貧民之賦可損，所謂損有餘補不足，令出而民利者也。

translations.literal: taking from surplus to supply upper use allows reducing poor people's taxes-the so-called reducing surplus to fill insufficiency, with policy issued and people benefiting.
translations.idiomatic: reallocating that surplus can reduce burdens on the poor and improve aggregate welfare.

sentences.zh: 順於民心，所補者三：一曰主用足，二曰民賦少，三曰勸農功。

translations.literal: Following people's hearts, what it benefits is threefold: first state use sufficient, second people's taxes fewer, third encouragement of farming achievement.
translations.idiomatic: The policy claims three gains at once: fiscal sufficiency, tax relief, and stronger farm incentives.

sentences.zh: 今令民有車騎馬一匹者，復卒三人。

translations.literal: Now if people have one warhorse and chariot, three persons are exempt from corvée soldiering.
translations.idiomatic: Current policy already grants labor exemptions for military assets like chariot-horse ownership.

sentences.zh: 車騎者，天下武備也，故為復卒。

translations.literal: Chariot and horse are military preparedness of the realm, therefore exemption is granted.
translations.idiomatic: Those exemptions are justified as supporting national defense readiness.

sentences.zh: 神農之教曰：「有石城十仞，湯池百步，帶甲百萬，而亡粟，弗能守也。」

translations.literal: Shennong's teaching says: 'With stone walls ten ren high, boiling moats one hundred paces wide, and a million armored troops-but without grain, one cannot hold it.'
translations.idiomatic: Classical doctrine emphasizes that logistics, not fortification alone, determines strategic survival.

sentences.zh: 以是觀之，粟者，王者大用，政之本務。

translations.literal: From this we see, grain is the king's great utility and government's fundamental task.
translations.idiomatic: So grain policy is not secondary-it is the central business of statecraft.

sentences.zh: 令民入粟受爵至五大夫以上，乃復一人耳，此其與騎馬之功相去遠矣。

translations.literal: Now allowing grain submission for rank up to rank Five-Dafu exempts only one person-this is far from the merit of one riding horse.
translations.idiomatic: Compared with military-asset exemptions, grain-for-rank incentives are still too weak.

sentences.zh: 爵者，上之所擅，出於口而亡窮；

translations.literal: Rank is what superiors control, issuing from the mouth without exhaustion;
translations.idiomatic: Ranks are politically cheap for the throne to grant;

sentences.zh: 粟者，民之所種，生於地而不乏。

translations.literal: grain is what people grow, born from earth and not lacking.
translations.idiomatic: while grain is socially expensive and materially indispensable.

sentences.zh: 夫得高爵與免罪，人之所甚欲也。

translations.literal: To obtain high rank and avoid punishment is what people greatly desire.
translations.idiomatic: People are powerfully motivated by status and penal relief.

sentences.zh: 使天下入粟於邊，以受爵免罪，不過三歲，塞下之粟必多矣。

translations.literal: If all under heaven submit grain at the frontier to receive rank and avoid punishment, in no more than three years grain below the passes will surely be abundant.
translations.idiomatic: A strong grain-for-rank program could fill frontier granaries within three years.

sentences.zh: 於是文帝從錯之言，令民入粟邊，六百石爵上造，稍增至四千石為五大夫，萬二千石為大庶長，各以多少級數為差。

translations.literal: Then Emperor Wen followed Cuo's words, ordering people to submit grain to frontiers: six hundred shi for rank Shangzao, gradually increasing to four thousand for Five Dafu, and twelve thousand for Da Shuzhang, each with graded levels by amount.
translations.idiomatic: Emperor Wen implemented a tiered grain-to-rank schedule with escalating thresholds for higher titles.

sentences.zh: 錯復奏言：「陛下幸使天下入粟塞下以拜爵，甚大惠也。

translations.literal: Cuo again memorialized: 'Your Majesty's fortunate policy letting the realm submit grain at frontier for rank is a great benefit.
translations.idiomatic: Cuo praised the measure as major policy innovation.

sentences.zh: 竊恐塞卒之食不足用大渫天下粟。

translations.literal: I fear frontier garrison food is still insufficient to use and release all-under-heaven grain.
translations.idiomatic: He warned that garrison consumption planning still needed refinement.

sentences.zh: 邊食足以支五歲，可令入粟郡縣矣；

translations.literal: When frontier food is enough for five years, then allow grain submission to commanderies and counties;
translations.idiomatic: Once five-year frontier reserves are secured, extend intake geographically inland;

sentences.zh: 足支一歲以上，可時赦，勿收農民租。

translations.literal: when enough for more than one year, one may occasionally grant amnesty and not collect peasant rent-tax.
translations.idiomatic: and where reserves exceed one-year needs, use temporary tax remissions to relieve peasant burdens.

sentences.zh: 如此，德澤加於萬民，民俞勤農。

translations.literal: If done so, virtue and beneficence extend to the myriad people, and people become more diligent in farming.
translations.idiomatic: Done this way, policy would deepen public welfare and strengthen agricultural effort.

sentences.zh: 時有軍役，若遭水旱，民不困乏，天下安寧；

translations.literal: When military service arose, or when flood and drought occurred, people were not distressed and exhausted, and the realm was at peace;
translations.idiomatic: Then even under mobilization or natural shocks, households would not collapse, and social order would hold.

sentences.zh: 歲孰且美，則民大富樂矣。」

translations.literal: if years were ripe and fine, the people became greatly wealthy and joyful.'
translations.idiomatic: And in good years, prosperity would become broad and visible.

sentences.zh: 上復從其言，乃下詔賜民十二年租稅之半。

translations.literal: The emperor again followed his words, issuing edict to remit half of year-12 rent and tax.
translations.idiomatic: The throne accepted this advice and granted a substantial tax reduction.

sentences.zh: 明年，遂除民田之租稅。

translations.literal: The next year, he removed field rent-tax entirely.
translations.idiomatic: A year later, land-tax was fully remitted.

sentences.zh: 後十三歲，孝景二年，令民半出田租，三十而稅一也。

translations.literal: Thirteen years later, in Jing year two, people were ordered to pay half field rent, taxing one in thirty.
translations.idiomatic: Later policy partially restored tax at a light one-thirtieth rate.

sentences.zh: 其後，上郡以西旱，復修賣爵令，而裁其賈以招民；

translations.literal: Afterward, when drought struck west of Shang commandery, the rank-selling order was again repaired, adjusting prices to attract people;
translations.idiomatic: In western drought conditions, rank-for-grain measures were revived with revised terms.

sentences.zh: 及徒復作，得輸粟於縣官以除罪。

translations.literal: and with convict labor restored, one could submit grain to county offices to remove penalties.
translations.idiomatic: Grain submission also became a channel for sentence commutation.

sentences.zh: 始造苑馬以廣用，宮室列館車馬益增修矣。

translations.literal: Parks for horses were first expanded for broader use, and palace halls, pavilions, carriages, and horses were increasingly augmented.
translations.idiomatic: At the same time, elite expenditure on court infrastructure and transport continued to expand.

sentences.zh: 然婁敕有司以農為務，民遂樂業。

translations.literal: Yet repeated imperial orders made agriculture the priority, and people thus delighted in their occupations.
translations.idiomatic: Even so, repeated pro-agriculture directives helped keep productive commitment relatively high.

sentences.zh: 至武帝之初七十年間，國家亡事，非遇水旱，則民人給家足，都鄙廩庾盡滿，而府庫餘財。

translations.literal: For seventy years to the start of Emperor Wu, the state had no major troubles; unless water or drought struck, households were provided for, granaries in capital and countryside full, and treasuries with surplus.
translations.idiomatic: Roughly seven decades of relative stability produced broad fiscal abundance and full granaries.

sentences.zh: 京師之錢累百鉅萬，貫朽而不可校。

translations.literal: Capital coin accumulated in hundreds of millions, with strings rotted and uncountable.
translations.idiomatic: Monetary surplus grew so large that cash strings physically decayed in storage.

sentences.zh: 太倉之粟陳陳相因，充溢露積於外，腐敗不可食。

translations.literal: Taicang grain piles layer on layer, overflowing and heaped outside, rotting unusable.
translations.idiomatic: State grain reserves became so excessive that some stock spoiled in open overflow storage.

sentences.zh: 眾庶街巷有馬，仟伯之間成群，乘牸牝者擯而不得會聚。

translations.literal: Common people in streets and lanes had horses in groups; those riding mares were shunned and not allowed to join gatherings.
translations.idiomatic: Livestock ownership had become so common that social distinction emerged even within horse ownership.

sentences.zh: 守閭閻者食粱肉；

translations.literal: Gate-and-lane keepers ate fine grain and meat;
translations.idiomatic: Even low urban functionaries consumed high-status diets;

sentences.zh: 為吏者長子孫；

translations.literal: those serving as officials grew their sons and grandsons in office;
translations.idiomatic: official families entrenched multigenerational office holding;

sentences.zh: 居官者以為姓號。

translations.literal: those residing in office took office-title as clan name.
translations.idiomatic: and office identity became hereditary social branding.

sentences.zh: 人人自愛而重犯法，先行誼而黜媿辱焉。

translations.literal: People cherished themselves and feared violating law, first practicing righteousness and rejecting shameful conduct.
translations.idiomatic: At this stage, social norms still favored propriety and legal caution.

sentences.zh: 於是罔疏而民富，役財驕溢，或至并兼豪黨之徒以武斷於鄉曲。

translations.literal: Then legal nets grew sparse while people rich; with wealth and labor arrogant and excessive, some reached annexing powerful cliques and using force to dominate local districts.
translations.idiomatic: Prosperity eventually bred private coercive networks and rural strongman politics.

sentences.zh: 宗室有土，公卿大夫以下爭於奢侈，室廬車服僭上亡限。

translations.literal: Imperial clans with lands, and from ministers downward, competed in luxury; houses, carriages, and dress usurped higher ranks without limit.
translations.idiomatic: Elite consumption escalated into status transgression across social strata.

sentences.zh: 物盛而衰，固其變也。

translations.literal: When things flourish they decline-this is fixed transformation.
translations.idiomatic: Prosperity's reversal is built into political cycles.

sentences.zh: 是後，外事四夷，內興功利，役費並興，而民去本。

translations.literal: After this, foreign affairs with border peoples and internal projects for profit both rose, service costs grew together, and people left the root.
translations.idiomatic: External campaigns and internal extraction jointly pulled society away from productive fundamentals.

sentences.zh: 董仲舒說上曰：「春秋它穀不書，至於麥禾不成則書之，以此見聖人於五穀最重麥與禾也。

translations.literal: Dong Zhongshu advised the emperor: 'In Spring and Autumn, other grains are not recorded, but when wheat and millet fail it is recorded-showing sages gave greatest weight among grains to wheat and millet.'
translations.idiomatic: Dong Zhongshu argued that classical texts singled out key staple failures as politically significant.

sentences.zh: 今關中俗不好種麥，是歲失春秋之所重，而損生民之具也。

translations.literal: 'Now Guanzhong customs do not like sowing wheat, so each year they lose what Spring and Autumn values and damage the means of people's life.'
translations.idiomatic: He criticized regional crop preferences that undermined staple resilience.

sentences.zh: 願陛下幸詔大司農，使關中民益種宿麥，令毋後時。」

translations.literal: 'May Your Majesty command the Grand Minister of Agriculture that Guanzhong people increase winter wheat and not miss seasonal timing.'
translations.idiomatic: He called for direct agronomic guidance to expand winter wheat.

sentences.zh: 又言：「古者稅民不過什一，其求易共；

translations.literal: He also said: 'In antiquity taxing people was no more than one-tenth, easy to provide;
translations.idiomatic: Classical fiscal norms, he said, kept extraction low and compliance feasible;

sentences.zh: 使民不過三日，其力易足。

translations.literal: using people's labor no more than three days, their strength easy to suffice.
translations.idiomatic: and labor demands were limited enough to preserve household capacity.

sentences.zh: 民財內足以養老盡孝，外足以事上共稅，下足以畜妻子極愛，故民說從上。

translations.literal: People's means were enough internally to care for elders and fulfill filiality, externally to serve superiors and taxes, below to support wives and children with full love, thus people gladly followed superiors.
translations.idiomatic: When household subsistence and obligations were simultaneously manageable, political obedience was voluntary.

sentences.zh: 至秦則不然，用商鞅之法，改帝王之制，除井田，民得賣買，富者田連仟伯，貧者亡立錐之地。

translations.literal: By Qin, not so: using Shang Yang's law, changing imperial kings' institutions, removing well-fields and allowing sale-purchase, rich fields stretched by tens and hundreds while poor had no ground to stand an awl.
translations.idiomatic: Qin reforms enabled severe land concentration and peasant landlessness.

sentences.zh: 又顓川澤之利，管山林之饒，荒淫越制，踰侈以相高；

translations.literal: It monopolized river-marsh profits and managed mountain-forest riches; wild excess surpassed norms, competing in extravagance.
translations.idiomatic: State and elite control over resource rents fueled escalating luxury and inequality.

sentences.zh: 邑有人君之尊，里有公侯之富，小民安得不困？

translations.literal: In towns there was lordly dignity, in villages marquis-level wealth-how could small people not be trapped?
translations.idiomatic: Sub-state elite power became so great that ordinary households had little room to survive.

sentences.zh: 又加月為更卒，已復為正，一歲屯戍，一歲力役，三十倍於古；

translations.literal: Further, adding monthly service as garrison labor and regular labor in sequence-one year frontier garrison, one year corvée-threefold ten times antiquity.
translations.idiomatic: Labor conscription expanded to levels far above classical precedent.

sentences.zh: 田租口賦，鹽鐵之利，二十倍於古。

translations.literal: Field rent and head tax, plus salt and iron revenues, were twenty times antiquity.
translations.idiomatic: The combined tax burden became vastly heavier than classical norms.

sentences.zh: 或耕豪民之田，見稅什五。

translations.literal: Some tilled powerful households' fields and were taxed at fifteen of ten.
translations.idiomatic: Tenancy under great households imposed predatory effective rates.

sentences.zh: 故貧民常衣牛馬之衣，而食犬彘之食。

translations.literal: Thus poor people often wore ox-and-horse clothing and ate dog-and-pig food.
translations.idiomatic: Peasant living standards degraded to near-animal subsistence.

sentences.zh: 重以貪暴之吏，刑戮妄加，民愁亡聊，亡逃山林，轉為盜賊，赭衣半道，斷獄歲以千萬數。

translations.literal: Added to this were greedy violent officials with arbitrary executions, people grieving without livelihood, fleeing into hills and forests turning to banditry, red convict clothes half the roads, and yearly prison judgments in tens of millions.
translations.idiomatic: Administrative violence plus poverty drove flight, crime, and mass penalization.

sentences.zh: 漢興，循而未改。

translations.literal: When Han arose, it followed this and had not changed.
translations.idiomatic: Early Han inherited much of this structure before reform.

sentences.zh: 古井田法雖難卒行，宜少近古，限民名田，以澹不足，塞并兼之路。

translations.literal: Though the old well-field law is hard to suddenly enact, one should somewhat approach antiquity: limit titled land holdings to relieve insufficiency and block annexation paths.
translations.idiomatic: Full restoration may be impossible, but partial anti-concentration land limits were urged.

sentences.zh: 鹽鐵皆歸於民。

translations.literal: Salt and iron should all return to the people.
translations.idiomatic: He advocated devolving salt and iron profits away from monopolistic control.

sentences.zh: 去奴婢，除專殺之威。

translations.literal: Remove slave categories and eliminate power of arbitrary killing.
translations.idiomatic: He also called for reducing coercive private domination.

sentences.zh: 薄賦斂，省繇役，以寬民力。

translations.literal: Lighten taxes and levies, reduce corvée, to relax people's strength.
translations.idiomatic: Tax and labor burdens should be reduced to restore social stamina.

sentences.zh: 然後可善治也。」

translations.literal: Only then can governance be good.
translations.idiomatic: Only under those conditions could stable good government emerge.

sentences.zh: 仲舒死後，功費愈甚，天下虛耗，人復相食。

translations.literal: After Zhongshu's death, expenditures and projects grew still greater; the realm emptied and consumed, people again ate one another.
translations.idiomatic: After his death, fiscal overextension intensified and extreme famine reappeared.

sentences.zh: 武帝末年，悔征伐之事，乃封丞相為富民侯。

translations.literal: At Emperor Wu's late years, he regretted campaigns and then enfeoffed the Chancellor as Marquis of Enriching the People.
translations.idiomatic: Late in reign, Emperor Wu signaled policy reversal by honoring 'enrich the people' governance.

sentences.zh: 下詔曰：「

translations.literal: He issued edict: '
translations.idiomatic: An edict declared:

sentences.zh: 方今之務，在於力農。」

translations.literal: At present the main task lies in strengthening agriculture.'
translations.idiomatic: 'The foremost task now is agricultural reinforcement.'

sentences.zh: 以趙過為搜粟都尉。

translations.literal: He made Zhao Guo Commandant for Grain Collection.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Guo was appointed to implement grain-oriented agrarian reform.

sentences.zh: 過能為代田，一畝三甽。

translations.literal: Guo could make alternating-field method, one mu with three furrows.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Guo introduced the three-furrow alternating-field technique.

sentences.zh: 歲代處，故曰代田，古法也。

translations.literal: Each year the position alternates, hence called alternating fields-an ancient method.
translations.idiomatic: Annual rotation gave the method its name and linked it to older agronomy.

sentences.zh: 后稷始甽田，以二耜為耦，廣尺深尺曰甽，長終畝。

translations.literal: Houji first furrowed fields, using two plows as a pair; width one chi and depth one chi was one furrow, running full mu length.
translations.idiomatic: The text traces furrow agriculture to Houji with paired-plow technique and fixed furrow dimensions.

sentences.zh: 一畝三甽，一夫三百甽，而播種於甽中。

translations.literal: One mu has three furrows; one man has three hundred furrows, with seed broadcast in furrows.
translations.idiomatic: The system quantified furrow units per laborer and sowing pattern.

sentences.zh: 苗生葉以上，稍耨隴草，因隤其土以附根苗。

translations.literal: When seedlings had leaves, they gradually weeded ridge grass and banked earth onto roots and seedlings.
translations.idiomatic: As seedlings grew, repeated weeding and hilling reinforced root stability.

sentences.zh: 故其詩曰：「或芸或芓，黍稷儗儗。」

translations.literal: Therefore its poem says: 'Some weed, some hoe; millet and broomcorn stand thick.'
translations.idiomatic: Classical verse is cited to describe labor rhythm and dense grain growth.

sentences.zh: 芸，除草也。

translations.literal: Yun means removing weeds.
translations.idiomatic: 'Yun' is glossed as weed removal.

sentences.zh: 刭，附根也。

translations.literal: Qin means attaching to roots.
translations.idiomatic: The technical term is glossed as hilling soil around roots.

sentences.zh: 言苗稍壯，每耨輒附根，比盛暑，隴盡而根深，能風與旱，故儗儗而盛也。

translations.literal: It means as seedlings gradually strengthen, each weeding adds root earth; by peak summer ridges are exhausted and roots deep, able against wind and drought, therefore lush and flourishing.
translations.idiomatic: Repeated hilling deepens rooting, improving resistance to wind and drought and producing robust growth.

sentences.zh: 其耕耘下種田器，皆有便巧。

translations.literal: Its plowing, weeding, and sowing implements all had convenient craftsmanship.
translations.idiomatic: The method depended on purpose-built, efficiency-oriented tools.

sentences.zh: 率十二夫為田一井一屋，故具五頃，用耦犁，二牛三人，一歲之收常過縵田具一斛以上，善者倍之。

translations.literal: Generally twelve men farm one well and one household block, thus furnishing five qing, using paired-plow, two oxen and three men; annual harvest commonly exceeds broad-field method by over one hu per mu, and good cases double it.
translations.idiomatic: At team scale, paired-plow cultivation produced significant yield gains over conventional broad-field farming.

sentences.zh: 過使教田太常、三輔，大農置工巧奴與從事，為作田器。

translations.literal: Guo had Grand Ritual, Three Adjacent Regions, and Grand Agriculture set craftsmen slaves and staff to make farming tools.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Guo coordinated central and regional agencies to mass-produce appropriate agricultural implements.

sentences.zh: 二千石遣令長、三老、力田及里父老善田者受田器，學耕種養苗狀。

translations.literal: Two-thousand-shi officials sent county heads, elder worthies, model farmers, and skilled village elders to receive tools and learn plowing, sowing, and seedling-care methods.
translations.idiomatic: Implementation used official plus local knowledge networks for technique transfer.

sentences.zh: 民或苦少牛，亡以趨澤，故平都令光教過以人輓犁。

translations.literal: Some people suffered from too few oxen and had no way to hurry cultivation, so Pingdu magistrate Guang taught Guo human-drawn plowing.
translations.idiomatic: Where draft animals were scarce, officials adapted by introducing human-drawn plows.

sentences.zh: 過奏光以為丞，教民相與庸輓犁。

translations.literal: Guo recommended Guang as assistant, teaching people to hire together for pulling plows.
translations.idiomatic: The adaptation was institutionalized through cooperative labor-pull arrangements.

sentences.zh: 率多人者田日三十畝，少者十三畝，以故田多墾闢。

translations.literal: Teams with many workers farmed thirty mu per day, fewer workers thirteen mu, therefore much more land was opened.
translations.idiomatic: Labor pooling sharply expanded daily tillage and total reclaimed acreage.

sentences.zh: 過試以離宮卒田其宮壖地，課得穀皆多其旁田畝一斛以上。

translations.literal: Guo tested with detached-palace soldiers farming palace perimeter lands; assessed grain was all over one hu per mu more than neighboring fields.
translations.idiomatic: Controlled trials on palace lands showed consistent yield advantage over adjacent plots.

sentences.zh: 令命家田三輔公田，又教邊郡及居延城。

translations.literal: Orders instructed household farming of Three Adjacent Regions public lands, and also taught border commanderies and Juyan city.
translations.idiomatic: The method was extended from core regions to frontier administrative zones.

sentences.zh: 是後邊城、河東、弘農、三輔、太常民皆便代田，用力少而得穀多。

translations.literal: After this, border cities, Hedong, Hongnong, Three Adjacent Regions, and Taichang people all found alternating fields convenient, using less labor and gaining more grain.
translations.idiomatic: Adoption spread widely because it reduced labor while raising output.

sentences.zh: 至昭帝時，流民稍還，田野益闢，頗有畜積。

translations.literal: By Emperor Zhao's time, migrants gradually returned, fields further opened, and there were somewhat accumulations.
translations.idiomatic: Under Zhao, population return and land reopening restored modest surplus capacity.

sentences.zh: 宣帝即位，用吏多選賢良，百姓安土，歲數豐穰，穀至石五錢，農人少利。

translations.literal: When Emperor Xuan ascended, he used officials often selecting worthy and good; people settled to land, years were often abundant, grain reached five cash per shi, and farmers gained little profit.
translations.idiomatic: Even in abundance under Xuan, very low grain prices squeezed farmer profitability.

sentences.zh: 時大司農中丞耿壽昌以善為算能商功利得幸於上，五鳳中奏言：「

translations.literal: Then Grand Agriculture vice-director Geng Shouchang, skilled in calculation and discussing utility, favored by the emperor, memorialized in Wufeng period:
translations.idiomatic: In Wufeng, fiscal technocrat Geng Shouchang proposed transport and storage reform.

sentences.zh: 故事，歲漕關東穀四百萬斛以給京師，用卒六萬人。

translations.literal: 'By old practice, yearly transporting four million hu from east of passes to supply the capital uses sixty thousand laborers.
translations.idiomatic: He argued long-distance tribute grain transport was labor-inefficient.

sentences.zh: 宜糴三輔、弘農、河東、上黨、太原郡穀足供京師，可以省關東漕卒過半。」

translations.literal: Better buy grain from Three Adjacent, Hongnong, Hedong, Shangdang, and Taiyuan to supply the capital, which can save more than half the eastern transport laborers.'
translations.idiomatic: Local-procurement substitution could cut transport labor by over half.

sentences.zh: 又白增海租三倍，天子皆從其計。

translations.literal: He also requested tripling sea-tax, and the Son of Heaven followed all his plans.
translations.idiomatic: He further proposed higher maritime tax rates, and the emperor approved.

sentences.zh: 御史大夫蕭望之奏言：「故御史屬徐宮家在東萊，言往年加海租，魚不出。

translations.literal: Censor-in-chief Xiao Wangzhi memorialized: 'Former censor subordinate Xu Gong's family in Donglai says in past years when sea tax was raised, fish did not come out.
translations.idiomatic: Xiao Wangzhi opposed, citing local testimony that overtaxing fisheries depressed catch.

sentences.zh: 長老皆言武帝時縣官嘗自漁，海魚不出，後復予民，魚乃出。

translations.literal: Elders all say that in Emperor Wu's time when county offices fished themselves, sea fish did not emerge; later returned to people, fish then emerged.
translations.idiomatic: He claimed direct state extraction had previously reduced fish supply, reversed when access returned to private fishers.

sentences.zh: 夫陰陽之感，物類相應，萬事盡然。

translations.literal: As for resonance of yin and yang, things respond by kind; all affairs are like this.
translations.idiomatic: He framed the argument in correlative cosmology: policy pressure can suppress natural-economic response.

sentences.zh: 今壽昌欲近糴漕關內之穀，築倉治船，費直二萬萬餘，有動眾之功，恐生旱氣，民被其災。

translations.literal: Now Shouchang wants nearby purchase and to stop transport to inside passes, building warehouses and repairing ships, costing over two hundred million; with mobilizing crowds, I fear producing drought qi and people suffering disaster.
translations.idiomatic: He warned that large infrastructure and mobilization costs could trigger social and climatic harm.

sentences.zh: 壽昌習於商功分銖之事，其深計遠慮，誠未足任，宜且如故。」

translations.literal: Shouchang is practiced in merchant-like calculations down to fractions; his deep planning and long foresight truly may not suffice-should temporarily remain as before.
translations.idiomatic: He characterized Shouchang as narrowly calculative and urged policy conservatism.

sentences.zh: 上不聽。

translations.literal: The emperor did not listen.
translations.idiomatic: The throne rejected that objection.

sentences.zh: 漕事果便，壽昌遂白令邊郡皆築倉，以穀賤時增其賈而糴，以利農，穀貴時減賈而糶，名曰常平倉。

translations.literal: Transport matters proved convenient; Shouchang then requested border commanderies all build warehouses, buying grain when cheap by raising official price to benefit farmers, and selling when expensive by lowering price, named Ever-Normal Granary.
translations.idiomatic: The policy worked, and Shouchang formalized the Ever-Normal system: buy high enough to support farmers in glut years, sell low enough to stabilize scarcity years.

sentences.zh: 民便之。

translations.literal: People found it convenient.
translations.idiomatic: The populace broadly welcomed the system.

sentences.zh: 上乃下詔，賜壽昌爵關內侯。

translations.literal: The emperor then issued edict granting Shouchang rank Guannei Marquis.
translations.idiomatic: Shouchang was rewarded with marquis rank for the reform.

sentences.zh: 而蔡癸以好農使勸郡國，至大官。

translations.literal: And Cai Kui, by loving agriculture, was sent to encourage commanderies and states, rising to high office.
translations.idiomatic: Agrarian advocacy also advanced other officials such as Cai Kui into major posts.

sentences.zh: 成帝時，天下亡兵革之事，號為安樂，然俗奢侈，不以畜聚為意。

translations.literal: In Chengdi's time the realm had no war affairs and was called peaceful and joyful, yet customs were extravagant and did not intend accumulation.
translations.idiomatic: Under Cheng there was peace but high-consumption culture and weak saving behavior.

sentences.zh: 永始二年，梁國、平原郡比年傷水災，人相食，刺史守相坐免。

translations.literal: In Yongshi year two, Liang state and Pingyuan commandery had repeated flood damage; people ate one another; inspectors, governors, and commandery heads were removed for culpability.
translations.idiomatic: Repeated flood famine caused cannibalism and administrative dismissals.

sentences.zh: 平帝崩，王莽居攝，遂篡位。

translations.literal: When Emperor Ping died, Wang Mang as regent then usurped the throne.
translations.idiomatic: After Ping's death, Wang Mang moved from regency to usurpation.

sentences.zh: 王莽因漢承平之業，匈奴稱藩，百蠻賓服，舟車所通，盡為臣妾，府庫百官之富，天下晏然。

translations.literal: Wang Mang inherited Han's pacified enterprise: Xiongnu as vassals, many southern peoples submitting, where boats and carts reached all became subjects, treasuries and offices wealthy, and the realm tranquil.
translations.idiomatic: He inherited a highly favorable geopolitical and fiscal baseline.

sentences.zh: 莽一朝有之，其心意未滿，骥小漢家制度，以為疏闊。

translations.literal: Mang possessed this in a single morning, yet his intentions were not satisfied; he despised Han institutions as loose and broad.
translations.idiomatic: Despite strong inheritance, he judged Han institutions inadequate and pursued radical redesign.

sentences.zh: 宣帝始賜單于印璽，與天子同，而西南夷鉤町稱王。
