translations.literal: Emperor Xuan first gave Chanyu seal matching the Son of Heaven's, and in southwest the Gou-ding ruler was called king.
translations.idiomatic: He began by reversing earlier diplomatic recognitions, including high-status seals and titles.

sentences.zh: 莽乃遣使易單于印，貶鉤町王為侯。

translations.literal: Mang then sent envoys to change the Chanyu seal and demoted Gou-ding king to marquis.
translations.idiomatic: These symbolic downgrades directly insulted frontier powers.

sentences.zh: 二方始怨，侵犯邊境。

translations.literal: The two regions then became resentful and invaded borderlands.
translations.idiomatic: Both frontiers reacted with hostility and cross-border aggression.

sentences.zh: 莽遂興師，發三十萬眾，欲同時十道並出，一舉滅匈奴；

translations.literal: Mang then raised armies, mobilizing three hundred thousand, wanting ten routes to move out simultaneously to destroy Xiongnu in one stroke;
translations.idiomatic: He planned a massive multi-column campaign for decisive destruction of the Xiongnu.

sentences.zh: 募發天下囚徒丁男甲卒轉委輸兵器，自負海江淮而至北邊，使者馳傳督趣，海內擾矣。

translations.literal: he recruited convicts and adult males and armored troops from all under heaven, transporting and delivering weapons from sea, river, and Huai to north frontier; envoys sped by relay to督促, and the realm was disturbed.
translations.idiomatic: This empire-wide mobilization and logistics drive overstrained society and destabilized the interior.

sentences.zh: 又動欲慕古，不度時宜，分裂州郡，改職作官，下令曰：「漢氏減輕田租，三十而稅一，常有更賦，罷癃咸出，而豪民侵陵，分田劫假，厥名三十，實什稅五也。

translations.literal: Again desiring to imitate antiquity without measuring present suitability, he split commanderies and altered offices, issuing order: 'Han reduced field rent, taxing one in thirty, with regular additional levies; even infirm and weak all paid, while wealthy households invaded and annexed fields by force and fake loans; nominally one-in-thirty, actually tax one-in-five.'
translations.idiomatic: He launched institutional overhauls framed as anti-inequality reform, while condemning Han's effective tax burden as much heavier in practice than nominal rates.

sentences.zh: 富者驕而為邪，貧者窮而為姦，俱陷於辜，刑用不錯。

translations.literal: The rich grew arrogant and became wicked, the poor desperate and became criminal; both fell into guilt, and punishments were never set aside.
translations.idiomatic: Class polarization, he claimed, drove both elite predation and mass criminalization.

sentences.zh: 今更名天下田曰王田，奴婢曰私屬，皆不得賣買。

translations.literal: Now rename all fields under heaven as King's Fields, and slaves as private dependents, all not allowed to buy or sell.
translations.idiomatic: He nationalized land nomenclature and banned buying/selling of land and bonded persons.

sentences.zh: 其男口不滿八，而田過一井者，分餘田與九族鄉黨。」

translations.literal: Where male mouths fewer than eight and fields exceed one well, divide surplus fields to nine-clan and local party.
translations.idiomatic: Households above land ceilings were ordered to redistribute excess to kin and community networks.

sentences.zh: 犯令，法至死，制度又不定，吏緣為姦，天下謷謷然，陷刑者眾。

translations.literal: Violating order reached death penalty; institutions were also unstable, officials exploited to commit wickedness, the realm clamored, and many fell into punishment.
translations.idiomatic: Harsh penalties plus unstable implementation generated widespread administrative abuse and social backlash.

sentences.zh: 後三年，莽知民愁，下詔諸食王田及私屬皆得賣買，勿拘以法。

translations.literal: Three years later, Mang knew people grieved and issued edict that all King's Fields and private dependents for subsistence could be bought and sold, not constrained by law.
translations.idiomatic: After three years of resistance, he partially reversed the prohibition and restored transactions.

sentences.zh: 然刑罰深刻，它政誖亂。

translations.literal: Yet punishments remained deeply harsh, and other policies disorderly and contradictory.
translations.idiomatic: Even after retreat, coercive legalism and policy incoherence persisted.

sentences.zh: 邊兵二十餘萬人仰縣官衣食，用度不足，數橫賦歛，民俞貧困。

translations.literal: Over two hundred thousand border troops depended on county offices for clothing and food; expenditures insufficient, horizontal exactions frequently imposed, and people became poorer and more distressed.
translations.idiomatic: Huge frontier maintenance costs drove repeated extraordinary levies, worsening mass impoverishment.

sentences.zh: 常苦枯旱，亡有平歲，穀賈翔貴。

translations.literal: They constantly suffered from severe drought, with no normal years, and grain prices soared high.
translations.idiomatic: Drought became chronic, normal harvest years disappeared, and grain prices shot upward.

sentences.zh: 末年，盜賊群起，發軍擊之，將吏放縱於外。

translations.literal: In the final years, bandits rose in groups; troops were dispatched to strike them, and generals and officials ran unchecked in the field.
translations.idiomatic: In the regime’s final years, uprisings spread, troops were sent out repeatedly, and commanders and officials operated with little restraint.

sentences.zh: 北邊及青徐地人相食，雒陽以東米石二千。

translations.literal: In the northern frontier and in Qing and Xu regions, people ate one another; east of Luoyang, rice reached two thousand per shi.
translations.idiomatic: Along the northern frontier and in Qing–Xu, famine became so extreme that cannibalism appeared, and east of Luoyang grain prices rose to two thousand per shi.

sentences.zh: 莽遣三公將軍開東方諸倉振貸窮乏，又分遣大夫謁者教民煮木為酪；

translations.literal: Mang sent the Three Dukes and generals to open eastern granaries to provide relief loans to the destitute, and also sent grand officers and court envoys to teach people to boil wood into gruel;
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mang ordered high officials to open eastern granaries for relief and sent envoys to instruct people in making emergency food from boiled wood.

sentences.zh: 酪不可食，重為煩擾。

translations.literal: That gruel was inedible, and instead added further disturbance.
translations.idiomatic: The so-called food was barely edible and only deepened the chaos.

sentences.zh: 流民入關者數十萬人，置養澹官以稟之，吏盜其稟，飢死者什七八。

translations.literal: Hundreds of thousands of displaced people entered the passes; offices were set up to feed them with rations, but officials stole those rations, and seven or eight in ten died of hunger.
translations.idiomatic: Hundreds of thousands of refugees entered the Guanzhong region. Relief offices were established, but officials embezzled the rations, and roughly seventy to eighty percent died of starvation.

sentences.zh: 莽恥為政所致，乃下詔曰：「予遭陽九之阨，百六之會，枯旱霜蝗，饑饉荐臻，蠻夷猾夏，寇賊姦軌，百姓流離。

translations.literal: Ashamed to admit it was caused by his governance, Mang then issued an edict saying: 'I have met the calamity of yang-nine and the conjunction of hundred-six; drought, frost, and locusts; repeated famine; barbarians troubling Xia; bandits and traitorous disorder; the common people are displaced.
translations.idiomatic: Ashamed to attribute the crisis to his own rule, Wang Mang issued an edict blaming cosmic misfortune and natural disasters: drought, frost, locusts, famine, frontier incursions, banditry, and mass displacement.

sentences.zh: 予甚悼之，害氣將究矣。」

translations.literal: I deeply grieve this; the harmful qi is about to run its course.'
translations.idiomatic: I grieve this deeply, and pray this baleful force is nearing its end.'

sentences.zh: 歲為此言，以至於亡。

translations.literal: Year after year he made such statements, until he perished.
translations.idiomatic: He repeated this kind of proclamation year after year, right up to the fall of his regime.
